# Application Reference

Use these objects to create an app, group routes by feature, and
configure Quater's built-in docs and safety defaults.

For the route model, read [Public API](/en/latest/api). For production
settings, read [Security](/en/latest/security).

```python
from quater import AppConfig, CORSConfig, Quater, RouteGroup
```

## Quater {#symbol-quater}

Public import: `from quater import Quater`.

The application object.

`Quater` owns the route registry, middleware, lifespan hooks, and
server adapters. A route can stay HTTP-only, or it can opt into MCP
and CLI surfaces with `tool=True` and `cli=True`.

```python
Quater(
    *,
    name: str | None = None,
    config: AppConfig | None = None,
    debug: bool | None = None,
    security: SecurityMode | None = None,
    allowed_hosts: Iterable[str] | None = None,
    trusted_proxies: Iterable[str] | None = None,
    max_body_size: MaxBodySize | None = None,
    cors: CORSConfig | None = None,
    content_security_policy: str | None = None,
    mcp_docs_path: str | None = '/mcp/docs',
    mcp_allowed_origins: Iterable[str] | None = None,
    mcp_auth: Authenticate | None = None,
    mcp_audit: AuditHook | None = None,
    cli_auth: Authenticate | None = None,
    action_approval: ActionApproval | None = None,
    access_logger: AccessLogHook | None = None,
    docs_path: str | None = '/docs',
    openapi_path: str | None = '/openapi.json',
    request_id_header: str | None = 'x-request-id',
)
```

### Constructor options

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `name` | `str \| None` | Optional app name used by generated metadata. |
| `config` | [`AppConfig`](/en/latest/reference/application#symbol-appconfig) \| None | Base [AppConfig](#symbol-appconfig) to start from. |
| `debug` | `bool \| None` | Override `config.debug`. Use only while developing. |
| `security` | [`SecurityMode`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-securitymode) \| None | Override the security mode. |
| `allowed_hosts` | `Iterable[str] \| None` | Hosts accepted by host checks. |
| `trusted_proxies` | `Iterable[str] \| None` | Proxy IPs trusted for forwarded headers. |
| `max_body_size` | [`MaxBodySize`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-maxbodysize) \| None | Maximum request body size. |
| `cors` | [`CORSConfig`](/en/latest/reference/application#symbol-corsconfig) \| None | Optional [CORSConfig](#symbol-corsconfig). |
| `content_security_policy` | `str \| None` | Optional CSP response header. |
| `mcp_docs_path` | `str \| None` | MCP docs path. `None` disables it. |
| `mcp_allowed_origins` | `Iterable[str] \| None` | Browser origins allowed for MCP. |
| `mcp_auth` | [`Authenticate`](/en/latest/reference/auth#type-authenticate) \| None | Required when any route has `tool=True`. See [Auth](./auth). |
| `mcp_audit` | [`AuditHook`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-audithook) \| None | Receives redacted [MCP audit events](./observability#symbol-toolauditevent). |
| `cli_auth` | [`Authenticate`](/en/latest/reference/auth#type-authenticate) \| None | Required when any route has `cli=True`. See [Auth](./auth). |
| `action_approval` | [`ActionApproval`](/en/latest/reference/auth#symbol-actionapproval) \| None | Required for protected tools/actions. See [ActionApproval](./auth#symbol-actionapproval). |
| `access_logger` | [`AccessLogHook`](/en/latest/reference/observability#symbol-accessloghook) \| None | Receives [structured access log events](./observability#symbol-accesslogevent). |
| `docs_path` | `str \| None` | Swagger UI path. `None` disables it. |
| `openapi_path` | `str \| None` | OpenAPI JSON path. `None` disables it. |
| `request_id_header` | `str \| None` | Correlation header name. `None` disables it. |

### Route decorators

Use decorators for normal route registration. `get`, `post`, `put`,
`patch`, and `delete` use the same options as `route`.

```python
route(
    method: str,
    path: str,
    *,
    name: str | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    tool: bool = False,
    cli: bool = False,
    needs_approval: bool = False,
    auth: Authenticate | None = None,
    metadata: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
    before: Iterable[BeforeMiddleware] = (),
    after: Iterable[AfterMiddleware] = (),
    around: Iterable[AroundMiddleware] = (),
    exception_handlers: Iterable[ExceptionHandlerEntry] = (),
) -> Callable[[HandlerT], HandlerT]
```

### Route options

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `method` | `str` | HTTP method. Only passed to `route()` or `add_route()`. |
| `path` | `str` | Route path, such as `/orders/{order_id}`. |
| `name` | `str \| None` | Stable operation name. Defaults to the handler name. |
| `description` | `str \| None` | Human text used by MCP tools, CLI actions, and docs. |
| `tool` | `bool` | Expose this route as an MCP tool. |
| `cli` | `bool` | Expose this route as a Quater CLI action. |
| `needs_approval` | `bool` | Require approval before MCP or CLI execution. |
| `auth` | [`Authenticate`](/en/latest/reference/auth#type-authenticate) \| None | Route-level auth hook. See [Auth](./auth). |
| `metadata` | `dict[str, Any] \| None` | Extra metadata used by docs and extensions. |
| `before` | Iterable[[`BeforeMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-beforemiddleware)] | Route before-request middleware. |
| `after` | Iterable[[`AfterMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-aftermiddleware)] | Route after-response middleware. |
| `around` | Iterable[[`AroundMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-aroundmiddleware)] | Route wrapper middleware. |
| `exception_handlers` | Iterable[[`ExceptionHandlerEntry`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-exceptionhandlerentry)] | Route-specific exception handlers. |

Descriptions matter for `tool=True` and `cli=True` routes. They are the
text an agent or operator sees before deciding whether to call the
operation.

### Common app methods

| Method | Use it for |
| --- | --- |
| `include(group)` | Include a [`RouteGroup`](#symbol-routegroup) in the app. |
| `add_route(...)` | Register a route without decorator syntax. |
| `before_request(...)` | Register global before-request middleware. |
| `after_response(...)` | Register global after-response middleware. |
| `around_request(...)` | Wrap the request handler pipeline. |
| `exception_handler(...)` | Register a global exception handler. |
| `on_startup(...)` / `on_shutdown(...)` | Register lifespan hooks. |
| `startup()` / `shutdown()` | Run lifespan hooks manually in tests. |
| `handle(request)` | Handle an in-process request. |
| `validate_production()` | Fail fast on unsafe production config. |

## RouteGroup {#symbol-routegroup}

Public import: `from quater import RouteGroup`.

A compile-time group for related routes.

A group lets you share a prefix, tags, auth, middleware, and exception
handlers across a feature area. Included groups are flattened into
normal routes before matching, so grouping does not add a router layer
to the hot path.

```python
RouteGroup(
    prefix: str = '',
    *,
    tags: Iterable[str] = (),
    auth: Authenticate | None = None,
    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    before: Iterable[BeforeMiddleware] = (),
    after: Iterable[AfterMiddleware] = (),
    around: Iterable[AroundMiddleware] = (),
    exception_handlers: Iterable[ExceptionHandlerEntry] = (),
)
```

### Constructor options

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `prefix` | `str` | Path prefix applied to child routes. |
| `tags` | `Iterable[str]` | OpenAPI tags inherited by child routes. |
| `auth` | [`Authenticate`](/en/latest/reference/auth#type-authenticate) \| None | Auth hook inherited by child routes. See [Auth](./auth). |
| `metadata` | `Mapping[str, Any] \| None` | Metadata inherited by child routes. |
| `before` | Iterable[[`BeforeMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-beforemiddleware)] | Before middleware inherited by routes. |
| `after` | Iterable[[`AfterMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-aftermiddleware)] | After middleware inherited by routes. |
| `around` | Iterable[[`AroundMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-aroundmiddleware)] | Around middleware inherited by routes. |
| `exception_handlers` | Iterable[[`ExceptionHandlerEntry`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-exceptionhandlerentry)] | Exception handlers inherited by routes. |

Use `app.include(group)` after all routes are declared. Quater locks the
group after inclusion so routes cannot silently disappear later.

Route groups expose the same route decorators as the app.

```python
route(
    method: str,
    path: str,
    *,
    name: str | None = None,
    description: str | None = None,
    tool: bool = False,
    cli: bool = False,
    needs_approval: bool = False,
    auth: Authenticate | None = None,
    metadata: Mapping[str, Any] | None = None,
    before: Iterable[BeforeMiddleware] = (),
    after: Iterable[AfterMiddleware] = (),
    around: Iterable[AroundMiddleware] = (),
    exception_handlers: Iterable[ExceptionHandlerEntry] = (),
) -> Callable[[HandlerT], HandlerT]
```

### Group route options

| Name | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `method` | `str` | HTTP method. Only passed to `route()` or `add_route()`. |
| `path` | `str` | Group-relative route path, such as `/{order_id}`. |
| `name` | `str \| None` | Stable operation name. Defaults to the handler name. |
| `description` | `str \| None` | Human text used by MCP tools, CLI actions, and docs. |
| `tool` | `bool` | Expose this route as an MCP tool. |
| `cli` | `bool` | Expose this route as a Quater CLI action. |
| `needs_approval` | `bool` | Require approval before MCP or CLI execution. |
| `auth` | [`Authenticate`](/en/latest/reference/auth#type-authenticate) \| None | Route-level auth hook. See [Auth](./auth). |
| `metadata` | `Mapping[str, Any] \| None` | Extra metadata inherited into the final route. |
| `before` | Iterable[[`BeforeMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-beforemiddleware)] | Route before-request middleware. |
| `after` | Iterable[[`AfterMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-aftermiddleware)] | Route after-response middleware. |
| `around` | Iterable[[`AroundMiddleware`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-aroundmiddleware)] | Route wrapper middleware. |
| `exception_handlers` | Iterable[[`ExceptionHandlerEntry`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-exceptionhandlerentry)] | Route-specific exception handlers. |

The route options mean the same thing on
[`RouteGroup`](#symbol-routegroup) as they do on
[`Quater`](#symbol-quater). Group-level auth, metadata, and
middleware are merged into the final route before the app compiles
routes.

## AppConfig {#symbol-appconfig}

Public import: `from quater import AppConfig`.

Immutable application configuration.

Most apps pass simple keyword overrides to
[`Quater(...)`](#symbol-quater). Use `AppConfig` when you want to
build configuration once and pass it around explicitly.

```python
AppConfig(
    debug: bool = False,
    security: SecurityMode = 'strict',
    allowed_hosts: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    trusted_proxies: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    max_body_size: int = 2 * 1024 * 1024,
    cors: CORSConfig | None = None,
    content_security_policy: str | None = None,
    docs_path: str | None = '/docs',
    openapi_path: str | None = '/openapi.json',
    mcp_docs_path: str | None = '/mcp/docs',
    mcp_allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    request_id_header: str | None = 'x-request-id',
)
```

Fields:

| Field | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `debug` | `bool` | Return detailed error responses while developing. |
| `security` | [`SecurityMode`](/en/latest/reference/application#type-securitymode) | `strict`, `relaxed`, or `off` security defaults. |
| `allowed_hosts` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Host names the app should accept. |
| `trusted_proxies` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Proxy IPs or CIDR ranges trusted for forwarded headers. |
| `max_body_size` | `int` | Maximum request body size in bytes. |
| `cors` | [`CORSConfig`](/en/latest/reference/application#symbol-corsconfig) \| None | Optional CORS policy. |
| `content_security_policy` | `str \| None` | Optional Content-Security-Policy header value. |
| `docs_path` | `str \| None` | Swagger UI path. Set to `None` to disable it. |
| `openapi_path` | `str \| None` | OpenAPI JSON path. Set to `None` to disable it. |
| `mcp_docs_path` | `str \| None` | Human-readable MCP docs path. Set to `None` to disable it. |
| `mcp_allowed_origins` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Origins allowed to call the MCP endpoint from browsers. |
| `request_id_header` | `str \| None` | Header used for incoming and outgoing request ids. |

## CORSConfig {#symbol-corsconfig}

Public import: `from quater import CORSConfig`.

Browser CORS policy.

CORS controls which browser origins may read responses. It is not an
authentication system; use `auth=...`, `mcp_auth`, and `cli_auth` for
access control.

```python
CORSConfig(
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...],
    allowed_methods: tuple[str, ...] = ('GET', 'POST', ...),
    allowed_headers: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    expose_headers: tuple[str, ...] = (),
    allow_credentials: bool = False,
    max_age: int | None = None,
)
```

Fields:

| Field | Type | Meaning |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `allowed_origins` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Origins allowed to read browser responses. |
| `allowed_methods` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Methods allowed during browser preflight checks. |
| `allowed_headers` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Request headers allowed during preflight. |
| `expose_headers` | `tuple[str, ...]` | Response headers browsers may expose to client code. |
| `allow_credentials` | `bool` | Whether browsers may include credentials. |
| `max_age` | `int \| None` | How long browsers may cache a preflight result. |

## __version__ {#symbol-version}

Public import: `from quater import __version__`.

Installed Quater version.

Use this for diagnostics or support output.

```python
__version__ = '0.1.0a1'
```

## Type Names Used Here

| Name | Meaning |
| --- | --- |
| <span id="type-securitymode"></span>`SecurityMode` | Literal config value: `strict`, `relaxed`, or `off`. |
| <span id="type-maxbodysize"></span>`MaxBodySize` | Either bytes as `int` or a string such as `"2mb"`. |
| <span id="type-authenticate"></span>`Authenticate` | Async auth hook. See [Auth and Security](./auth#symbol-authrequest). |
| <span id="type-actionapproval"></span>[`ActionApproval`](/en/latest/reference/auth#symbol-actionapproval) | Async approval hook. See [ActionApproval](./auth#symbol-actionapproval). |
| <span id="type-audithook"></span>`AuditHook` | Async MCP audit hook receiving [ToolAuditEvent](./observability#symbol-toolauditevent). |
| <span id="type-accessloghook"></span>[`AccessLogHook`](/en/latest/reference/observability#symbol-accessloghook) | Async access-log hook. See [Observability](./observability#symbol-accessloghook). |
| <span id="type-beforemiddleware"></span>`BeforeMiddleware` | Runs before the handler. It can short-circuit by returning a response. |
| <span id="type-aftermiddleware"></span>`AfterMiddleware` | Runs after the handler and can adjust the response. |
| <span id="type-aroundmiddleware"></span>`AroundMiddleware` | Wraps the handler pipeline for timing, tracing, or similar cross-cutting behavior. |
| <span id="type-exceptionhandlerentry"></span>`ExceptionHandlerEntry` | Internal wrapper for exception handlers passed through decorators. |
